name: warm-day-setback-cool-mode
description: Warm day + thermostat in cool mode: setback_cool applied instead of HVAC off (Issue #96)
notes: Issue #96: On a warm day the classifier sets hvac_mode='off', but when the thermostat
is currently running in cool mode the correct behavior is to apply the setback_cool
temperature (82F) rather than issuing a full HVAC-off command.
This allows the thermostat to coast naturally — AC shuts off when indoor temp falls
below 82F rather than being forced off immediately.
The indoor temp (74F) is above comfort_heat (70F) so the warm-day comfort-gap guard
does NOT fire — the system proceeds directly to setback_cool.
Expected outcome at classification time: setback_applied with target_temp=82.0.
HVAC mode must remain 'cool' — set_hvac_mode('off') must NOT be called.

events.note: Morning temps: indoor 74F (above comfort_heat 70F), outdoor 68F on a warm day
Classifier issues warm-day hvac_mode=off. Thermostat is in cool mode. Expected: setback_cool (82F) applied, no mode change.

assertions.reason: warm day + thermostat in cool mode — setback_cool (82F) applied instead of HVAC off

verdict.summary: Warm-day classifier respects thermostat cool mode — applies setback instead of shutoff
